skill.name: machine-learning-ops-ml-pipeline
skill.description: Guide an AI assistant through planning a production-ready MLOps pipeline for a machine learning project.
skill.summary: Plan data, training, deployment, monitoring, and operations for ML pipelines.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found no executable code, shell command, cryptographic operation, or reconnaissance instruction. The findings are false positives caused by Markdown text and MLOps terminology. A non-security quality issue remains because the skill references a resource file that is not present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Resource Reference
low_findings.description: The line tells the assistant to open a local Markdown playbook when examples are needed. This is not shell execution, but the referenced file is absent from the scanned file tree.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is a Markdown instruction to open a local documentation file, not an executable command. No shell syntax, command runner, or script file exists in the scanned evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: MLOps Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged lines containing machine learning and operations terminology as weak cryptography. No hash function, cipher, key handling, or cryptographic API is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose or task prompts about ML pipeline design. They do not contain cryptographic algorithm names, code, imports, or function calls.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Deployment Mode Option Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The flagged line lists valid deployment mode choices for ML pipelines. It does not request host discovery, network scanning, account enumeration, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a configuration option list item, not an instruction to inspect a system. No reconnaissance command or data collection workflow appears near the line.

content.value_statement: Production ML systems need coordinated data, training, deployment, and monitoring plans. This skill structures an MLOps workflow into clear phases and deliverables.
actual_capabilities: Breaks an ML pipeline project into data, training, deployment, Kubernetes, and monitoring phases.
Defines deliverables for data quality, feature engineering, experiment tracking, and model registry workflows.
Guides deployment planning for REST APIs, batch jobs, streaming inference, and model serving platforms.
Outlines Kubernetes workload, storage, autoscaling, and service mesh considerations for ML workloads.
Specifies monitoring areas for model quality, drift, system metrics, alerts, and cost tracking.
limitations: It is a planning and orchestration guide, not a runnable pipeline generator by itself.
It references a resource file that is not included in the scanned package.
Generated implementation details still need validation against real data, infrastructure, and security requirements.
It assumes access to specialized subagents or equivalent expert roles.

use_cases.title: Plan a New ML Platform
use_cases.description: Create a phased architecture for data ingestion, feature storage, model training, deployment, and monitoring.

use_cases.title: Standardize Model Delivery
use_cases.description: Define repeatable deliverables for experiment tracking, registry promotion, CI/CD gates, and rollback procedures.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Readiness Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the success criteria and monitoring checklist to evaluate launch readiness before production release.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Pipeline Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new model project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design an MLOps pipeline for a customer churn model. Include data sources, training, deployment, and monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Choose MLOps Tooling
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare MLflow, Feast, KServe, Kubeflow, and Prometheus for a fraud detection pipeline on Kubernetes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Production Handoffs
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create phase handoffs for data engineers, data scientists, ML engineers, MLOps engineers, and observability engineers.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Launch Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review a near-production recommendation model pipeline for gaps in validation, rollback, drift detection, and cost controls.

output_examples.input: Design an MLOps pipeline for a real-time fraud model.
output: A phased plan covering streaming ingestion, feature validation, model training, registry promotion, canary deployment, and drift monitoring.
A deliverables list for data engineering, ML engineering, Kubernetes, and observability owners.

output_examples.input: Prepare a production readiness checklist for an image classification model.
output: A checklist for data quality gates, reproducible training runs, model registry metadata, autoscaling, rollback, latency alerts, and cost limits.
A review summary that separates launch blockers from post-launch improvements.

best_practices: Start with data quality, lineage, and reproducibility before selecting model serving infrastructure.
Define promotion gates, rollback rules, and monitoring thresholds before production deployment.
Map each deliverable to an owner so phase handoffs are clear and testable.
anti_patterns: Do not treat experiment notebooks as production training pipelines without tests and configuration management.
Do not deploy models without drift detection, rollback procedures, and operational alerts.
Do not choose tools before defining latency, scale, governance, and team ownership requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill create runnable code?
faq.answer: It asks for implementation code in several phases, but the skill itself is an orchestration guide.

faq.question: Which tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It references MLflow, Weights and Biases, Feast, Tecton, KServe, Seldon, Kubeflow, Airflow, Terraform, Helm, and monitoring stacks.

faq.question: Can it support batch and real-time inference?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes realtime APIs, batch prediction, streaming pipelines, and hybrid deployment modes.

faq.question: Is it tied to one cloud provider?
faq.answer: No. It lists AWS, Azure, GCP, and multi-cloud options, but users must adapt details to their environment.

faq.question: What teams benefit most from it?
faq.answer: MLOps, platform, data engineering, data science, and observability teams can use it to coordinate production delivery.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing generated work?
faq.answer: They should verify security controls, data access, secrets handling, compliance needs, cost estimates, and production test results.
